title: senecio atropurpureus var. tomentosus
intro: Senecio atropurpureus var. tomentosus, also known as woolly purple ragwort, is a variety of the Senecio atropurpureus plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and flowers of Senecio atropurpureus var. tomentosus have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. When consumed in moderation, the plant's herbal preparations have been reported to have beneficial effects on overall health, including reducing the severity of respiratory symptoms, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
